description_long: This is a military-style, four-engine propeller airplane with a camouflage paint scheme, reminiscent of World War II bombers such as the Avro Lancaster. The fuselage is long and cylindrical, with a rounded nose and a glass canopy cockpit positioned at the front. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, extending widely with roundels and camouflage patterns visible on the upper surfaces. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, and the aircraft bears military insignia and identification markings. Four large propeller engines are mounted on the wings, two on each side, and the undercarriage appears to be retracted or not visible. The overall construction suggests a robust metal airframe with glass for the cockpit and some rubber components for tires and seals.
description: A large, camouflaged, four-engine propeller military airplane with a mid-mounted straight wing and conventional tail, featuring a glass cockpit and military markings.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the camouflage pattern, roundels on the wings, and the full span of the mid-mounted wings.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the wide wingspan, four propeller engines, and the tail's conventional configuration.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view displaying the underwing structure, engine placement, and the tail section with visible markings.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the four propeller engines, glass cockpit, and the symmetrical wing layout.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines, nacelle-mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable landing gear, likely tricycle configuration, though gear is not visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-seat glass canopy at the nose of the fuselage.
